findings.title: Delegation protocol content resides in use-hivemind instead of use-hivemind-delegation

evidence.content_summary: Multi-wave dispatch, topology selection, parallel dispatch rules, handoff packet format, wave flow diagrams, agent selection, escalation rules

findings.detail: delegation-protocol.md contains deep delegation mechanics (wave flows, topology, parallel rules, handoff packets) that belong in use-hivemind-delegation, not use-hivemind. use-hivemind is the entry router - it should route TO delegation, not contain delegation methodology.
findings.impact: Conceptual contamination - use-hivemind contains implementation details of a domain it merely routes to

findings.title: Batch loading template resides in use-hivemind instead of use-hivemind-delegation

evidence.content_summary: Dynamic skill batch composition, layer definitions, conditional batch templates (TDD, planning, debugging, research), multi-wave rotation rules

findings.detail: load-template.md describes batch rotation across waves, skill prerequisite chains, and conditional loading patterns. This is delegation coordination logic, not entry routing logic. The orchestrator mandate reference (lines 172-188) even states 'Skills are loaded in conditional batches' which is delegation territory.
findings.impact: Batch composition and rotation is a delegation concern, not a routing concern

findings.title: Intent classification is absent from the SKILL.md routing logic

evidence.gap: Step 4 says 'Conversation Context Assessment' but provides no explicit intent classification mechanism

findings.detail: The user explicitly requested 'intent' as a core function. The SKILL.md has no intent classification table, signal-word matching, or request type taxonomy. The Routing Decision Table exists but has no documentation on HOW to classify a request into those categories. Compare with use-hivemind-research which has explicit classification tables.
findings.impact: No documented mechanism for determining user intent before routing

findings.title: Context rot defense mechanisms documented in reference but not in SKILL.md

evidence.gap: Distrust levels (CLEAN/SUSPECT/DEGRADED/POLLUTED/POISONED) and context rot detection triggers exist in reference but SKILL.md only has a 5-step gate with no rot-specific countermeasures

findings.detail: context-health-protocol.md has thorough context rot detection (triggers, distrust levels, recovery actions) but this is not summarized or referenced as a required step in the SKILL.md's Context Integrity Gate. The gate mentions 'stale/corrupt state' but doesn't define the levels or triggers.
findings.impact: Context rot defense is buried in a reference file, not surfaced in the primary SKILL.md

findings.title: Role detection described in frontmatter but detection logic is in reference file

evidence.gap: The actual detection logic (how to determine if agent is hiveminder or hivefiver) is in orchestrator-self-assessment.md lines 46-62, not in the SKILL.md itself

findings.detail: The SKILL.md frontmatter promises role detection but the actual detection protocol is in a reference file. For a mandatory first-action gate, the detection logic should be in the SKILL.md itself, not deferred to a reference.
findings.impact: Minor - detection logic exists but is not immediately visible in SKILL.md

broken_links_check.details: All 7 reference files and 1 template file listed in SKILL.md exist and are readable. No orphaned references detected.

implemented.routing_hub: ✅ Present - Routing Decision Table with 10 categories
implemented.governance_gates: ✅ Present - Context Integrity Gate (5 steps) + Anti-Patterns Summary
implemented.intent: ❌ MISSING - No intent classification mechanism in SKILL.md

structure_match_assessment.verdict: Partially matches user requirements - routing and governance are present, but intent classification is absent

wrong_content_location.reason: Contains delegation methodology (wave dispatch, topology, handoffs) not entry routing
Batch rotation and skill composition across waves is delegation coordination

wrong_content_location.content: Multi-wave dispatch patterns section in orchestrator-mandate.md
wrong_content_location.reason: Mandate references delegation protocol for full dispatch rules - the full rules belong in delegation skill

missing_content.where_should_be: SKILL.md Context Integrity Gate or its own reference
missing_content.detail: No signal-word matching, no request type taxonomy for routing decisions
Distrust levels and rot triggers exist in context-health-protocol.md but not summarized in SKILL.md
Detection logic is in orchestrator-self-assessment.md, not immediately visible in SKILL.md

top_issues: Delegation protocol content (232 lines) should be in use-hivemind-delegation, not use-hivemind
Batch loading template (188 lines) should be in use-hivemind-delegation
Intent classification is entirely absent from SKILL.md
Context rot defense levels/triggers not surfaced in SKILL.md main body
summary_for_orchestrator.verdict: SKILL.md is structurally sound as entry router but contains delegation methodology that should be moved. Intent and context rot content is missing from main SKILL.md.
